travelPaceToolbox.summary: Terrain and circumstances can limit or improve overland travel pace.

backgroundCreation.summary: A custom background can be built to match the campaign world or a player’s concept.

steps.summary: Choose three abilities appropriate to the background.
Choose one feat from the Origin category.
Choose two skills appropriate for the background.
Choose one tool associated with the background.
Assemble a package of equipment worth 50 GP, including unspent gold. Do not include Martial weapons or armor.

cursesAndMagicalContagions.summary: A curse is a magical burden; a magical contagion is an adverse effect of magical origin that is contagious by definition.

curseTypes.label: Bestow Curse
curseTypes.summary: A straightforward benchmark curse with limited effects, removable by Remove Curse.

curseTypes.label: Cursed Creatures
curseTypes.summary: Some monsters are defined by or spread curses. Remove Curse interaction is up to the GM.

curseTypes.label: Cursed Magic Items
curseTypes.summary: Some magic items are cursed by design or supernatural origin.

curseTypes.label: Narrative Curses
curseTypes.summary: Story-driven curses tied to taboos, sacrilege, betrayal, or similar wrongs.

curseTypes.label: Environmental Curses
curseTypes.summary: Some places are so saturated with malign power that those who remain there become cursed.

magicalContagion.summary: Adverse magical effects that spread contagiously.

environmentalEffects.summary: Environmental conditions can alter checks, movement, attacks, and survival pressure.

effects.label: Frigid Water
effects.summary: Falling into or remaining in dangerously cold water is hazardous.

effects.label: Heavy Precipitation
effects.summary: Can obscure vision and worsen perception and movement conditions.

effects.label: Strong Wind
effects.summary: Imposes Disadvantage on ranged weapon attacks, extinguishes open flames, disperses fog, and forces flying creatures to land or fall at the end of their turn.

effects.label: Thin Ice
effects.summary: Thin ice has a variable weight tolerance; exceeding it breaks the ice and drops creatures through into frigid water.

fearAndMentalStress.summary: Fear effects use Frightened as a baseline. Mental stress is usually best represented by Psychic damage.

sampleFearDCs.example: A harmless yet terrifying apparition appears when the characters open a sarcophagus.
A magical trap creates an illusory manifestation of a character’s worst fears, visible only to that character.
A portal to the Abyss opens, revealing a nightmarish realm of torment and slaughter.

sampleEffects.example: A character ingests a hallucinogenic substance that distorts perception of reality.
A character touches a fiendish idol that tears at the mind, threatening to shatter it.
A magical trap flings a character into the Far Realm until the end of that character’s next turn.

poison.summary: Poisons are dangerous special substances with different delivery methods.

types.label: Contact
types.summary: Smeared on an object and remains potent until touched or washed off.

types.label: Ingested
types.summary: A creature must swallow an entire dose to suffer the effects.

types.label: Inhaled
types.summary: Delivered through breathing in powder, vapor, gas, or similar poison.

types.label: Injury
types.summary: Applied through a wound, often via a weapon, trap, bite, or sting.

traps.summary: Traps work best as brief obstacles or tense puzzles rather than unfair, inescapable punishment.

severity.label: Nuisance
severity.summary: Unlikely to seriously harm characters of the indicated levels.

severity.label: Deadly
severity.summary: Can grievously damage characters of the indicated levels.

traps.gmWarning: A trap that is a nuisance at one level range might be deadly to characters below that range.

exampleTrap.name: Collapsing Roof
exampleTrap.trigger: a creature crosses a trip wire

combatEncounters.summary: This section exists in the Gameplay Toolbox and should be expanded later into structured encounter-building support.
